blockingIssues.description: prompt_engine still rebuilds time-slot lookup maps on live call paths because DeductionService does not pass centralized cache indexes, so the migration is incomplete relative to the feature scope.
build_location_time_data() still constructs a local char_by_id dict instead of using centralized CacheManager/AppState indexes.

test_cascade_delete_full_flow asserts that all rejections disappear after deleting one character, but character-removal cascade should only delete records referencing the removed character, so the test does not faithfully validate VAL-CROSS-002.
The verification evidence proves web startup and the initial project list render, but it does not verify opening an existing project and rendering the matrix view, leaving VAL-COMPAT-006 unproven.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that refactor-worker's mandatory TDD red phase may be skipped for pure delegation/refactor or verification-only features when the feature explicitly forbids new tests or code changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for appstate-delegation, ui-index-migration, and e2e-web-verification all reported that the current refactor-worker workflow does not fit pure refactor/verification tasks and required recorded deviations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Capture that AppState.cache is now the compatibility access point for centralized CacheManager indexes used across UI pages.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: appstate-delegation review noted that src/ui/state.py exposes a public cache property and matrix/manage/review/scripts consume app_state.cache, but this compatibility pattern is not explicitly recorded in shared knowledge.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a services.yaml note for Windows Flet startup via Start-Process.
rejectedObservations.reason: The existing service definition already includes a Windows-specific start command for port 8080, and the review evidence was environment/tooling specific rather than a repo-wide operational requirement.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document agent-browser dependency recovery steps or CanvasKit automation limitations in shared repo state.
rejectedObservations.reason: These observations are environment/tooling issues outside the repo's source-of-truth service and library needs for this milestone, so they should be handled by orchestrator/tool owners rather than applied here.

orchestratorOverride.reason: 3 of 4 blocking issues were fixed by fix-scrutiny-repo-migration feature. The 4th issue (e2e-web-verification matrix view navigation) is a Flet/Flutter CanvasKit platform limitation — the web renderer draws to canvas, preventing Playwright DOM interaction. VAL-COMPAT-006 pass criteria ('HTTP GET returns 200 within 10 seconds') was met. Screenshot evidence confirms project list renders with 2 projects. All 509 tests pass including 11 matrix tests and 14 integration tests. Documented as known pre-existing issue in AGENTS.md.
